stories.headline: Iran war live: Vance arrives in Pakistan to lead ceasefire talks

stories.summary: Talks due in Washington as Israeli attacks on Lebanon continue, Iran says ceasefire must include them.

stories.headline: Frontrunner for California governor denies sexual assault allegations
stories.summary: The congressman is a leading Democratic candidate who is now facing calls to drop out of the race.

stories.headline: EU airline industry warns of fuel shortages if Strait of Hormuz stays closed
stories.summary: Airports Council International (ACI) Europe said its members had "increasing concerns" about the availability of jet fuel.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell Faces Sex Assault Accusation as He Runs for California Governor
stories.summary: Eric Swalwell, a Democratic congressman, denied the accounts published by CNN and The San Francisco Chronicle. Nancy Pelosi was among those who said he should withdraw from the California governor’s race.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell, California governor candidate, accused of sexual assault by ex-staffer
stories.summary: Supporters of Rep. Eric Swalwell’s campaign withdrew support, including two congressmen who chaired his campaign and two senators who endorsed his bid. Swalwell denied the allegations.

stories.headline: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
stories.summary: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

stories.headline: Odd Lots: The Big Macro Force That’s Kept Stocks High (Podcast)
stories.summary: Stocks have gone up over the years because corporate earnings continue to grow. That part is straightforward. But in addition to rising stock prices, we’ve also seen rising stock market valuations. For years, investors have talked about stocks being unreasonably priced, and yet they haven’t reverted to historical norms. But perhaps there’s a good explanation for this, beyond just animal spirits. Jonathan Heathcote is an economist at the Minneapol

stories.headline: Global Funds Flee Indian Stocks at Record Pace on Growth Fears
stories.summary: Global funds are dumping Indian equities at a record clip as an energy shock from the US-Iran war threatens to derail the outlook of the world’s fastest-growing major economy.

stories.headline: Djibouti’s President Guelleh wins sixth straight term in office
stories.summary: The longtime ruler, in power since 1999, has secured 97 percent of the vote, according to official results.

stories.headline: Europe Energy Traders Brace for 21-Hour Day as Volatility Surges
stories.summary: European gas and power markets, once a niche corner of global energy trading, are in for a major change next week: Hours will more than double to 21 from 10, ending the narrow daytime window that has been the norm for years.

stories.summary: How inequality has helped keep the stock market boom alive.

stories.headline: African Development Bank on Continent's Water Future
stories.summary: The African Development Bank says water investments on the continent have increased over the past decade — from upstream preparation of projects to desalination efforts, service delivery and wastewater treatment plants. But Mtchera Chirwa, the AfDB's water and sanitation department director, says gaps within the sector won't just be resolved with more capital injection. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: IOI Properties Files REIT Listing That May Raise $500 Million
stories.summary: IOI Properties Group Bhd. plans to establish a real estate investment trust holding its retail, hotel, and office assets, followed by a listing that could raise about 1.98 billion ringgit ($500 million) for the Malaysian developer.

stories.headline: Kalshi Wins Reprieve From Arizona Criminal Case at CFTC’s Urging
stories.summary: Kalshi Inc. won a temporary reprieve from criminal prosecution in Arizona when a judge suspended the case at the behest of a federal regulator.

stories.headline: As Election Looms, Will Alito Retire, Giving Trump a New Supreme Court Pick?
stories.summary: Justice Samuel A. Alito Jr. is the subject of intense speculation about whether he will retire in the coming months and give President Trump a fourth nominee.

stories.headline: Zebras, wealth and power: Hungary’s election tests Orbán’s grip on power
stories.summary: Corruption scandals and a surging opposition have turned the vote into the biggest test yet for the long-serving populist leaderThe drone footage showed a sprawling residence in northern Hungary, complete with manicured gardens, a swimming pool and an underground garage. But it was what came next that captured much of the country’s imagination: zebras darting across the countryside. Continue reading...

stories.headline: What’s at stake in Benin’s presidential election?
stories.summary: Finance minister is favourite to win on Sunday as incumbent Talon will step down after leading the country for 10 years.

stories.headline: Pakistan’s prime minister calls US-Iran talks ‘make or break’

stories.summary: Hungary elections: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe, writes Gerolf Annemans of the “Patriots for Europe” group in the European Parliament in an op-ed for Euronews. Annemans says Orbán has become a symbol of resistance to centralisation.

stories.headline: House prices across Europe: Which countries saw the highest rises in 2025?
stories.summary: House prices rose by 5.5% in the EU in the last quarter of 2025, with stronger growth in some tourist-attractive countries such as Portugal, Croatia and Spain. Experts explain the differences between countries for Euronews Business.

stories.headline: JD Vance warns Iran not to 'play' US during planned talks in Pakistan

stories.headline: Haass: Iran Has Far More Leverage Than Before War
stories.summary: The US is expected to hold talks with Iran in an effort to reach an agreement, but former Council on Foreign Relations president Richard Haass of Centerview Partners warns that we should not expect too much, and that the war shows just how much leverage Iran has. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump started the Iran war with 5 goals. How far has he gotten?
stories.summary: The president claimed he achieved them all. The reality is more complicated.

stories.headline: Israel: Hezbollah rocket from Lebanon strikes Safed, injuring several people
stories.summary: Emergency services were deployed in Safed on 10 April 2026 after a rocket fired from Lebanon by Hezbollah struck the northern city, prompting a rapid response to treat casualties and secure the area.

stories.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patronThe Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded.The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Airport council warns: Hormuz closure poses major fuel shortage risks
stories.summary: In a letter to the EU, the Airports Council International warned that if Hormuz does not reopen within three weeks, paraffin shortages in Europe will be critical.

stories.headline: Dutch police urge victims of 'international sextortion campaign' to come forward

stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

stories.headline: Burned Before, Democrats Abruptly Revive the Idea of Impeaching Trump
stories.summary: Democrats in Congress have assiduously avoided talk of a third impeachment of President Trump, concerned that it would distract from their midterm campaign message. That tide seems to have turned.

stories.headline: Officials Release Design for 250-Foot Arch in Washington, as Trump Seeks Another Imprint
stories.summary: The president has proposed the arch, which would rise on a Washington roundabout across from the Lincoln Memorial, as a way to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary.

stories.headline: Eliot Engel, a prominent voice on foreign affairs in the House, dies at 79
stories.summary: The New York Democrat helped lead the first impeachment inquiry into President Donald Trump. He served for 16 terms before losing his reelection bid in 2020.

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz falls outside NATO's remit, Spain says after Trump's ultimatum

stories.headline: Irish government prepares fuel support deal after blockade pressure
stories.summary: Travel across parts of the Republic of Ireland was affected for the fourth day in a row as vehicles, including tractors, blocked roads.

stories.headline: Colombia Hits Back at Ecuador With 100% Reciprocal Tariffs
stories.summary: Colombia announced retaliatory tariffs of 100% on some imports from Ecuador, deepening tensions between the ideologically opposed governments.

stories.headline: Fed Seeks Details on US Banks’ Exposure to Private Credit Firms
stories.summary: The Federal Reserve is asking major US banks for details about their exposure to private credit following a surge in redemptions from the funds and a rise in troubled loans in the industry, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

stories.headline: Trump Moves Top Aide James Blair to Political Operation Ahead of Midterms
stories.summary: Mr. Blair will “lead the charge from the outside,” the president said on social media. The move comes as Republicans are bracing for a potentially difficult November election.

stories.headline: Colombia responds to Ecuador’s tariff hike with 100-percent import tax
stories.summary: The tit-for-tat tariff increase comes amid cross-border tension over illicit drugs and fate of politician Jorge Glas.

stories.headline: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.summary: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.headline: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
